scope: Export-planning metadata only. No design files are created or moved by this manifest.

audit_findings.message: Task context says `IOS_SURFACE_INVENTORY.json` contains 13 surfaces, but the file currently contains 12 entries.
The compliance audit adds `Profile iOS` as a 13th documented surface via repo brief, but that surface is still missing from `IOS_SURFACE_INVENTORY.json`.
The migration plan's current surface inventory now includes `Race Prep — cards (summary)` as item `1b`, but `IOS_SURFACE_INVENTORY.json` still omits that canonical summary surface.

The migration plan references five additional canonical handoff surfaces not present in the JSON inventory: `Race Log iOS`, `Profile iOS`, `Discover-Orgs iOS`, `Discover-People iOS`, and `Discover-Forums iOS`.

Cutover state is stale across sources: the JSON inventory still marks `race-prep-ios` as `parked`, while the migration plan says the paired `Race Prep cards iOS` handoff landed and shipped on 2026-05-15.

Task context says 16 canonical iOS register designs live only in Claude Design, but the cross-referenced docs imply 18 distinct surface-level export targets if every canonical surface and backlog handoff is treated separately. This manifest keeps all 18 visible rather than silently reconciling the count.

exports.source: Claude Design — Race Prep iOS detail surface (Pass A — iOS; `Race Prep - Race 4 - iOS register.html`)
exports.notes: Inventory marks canonical_status=`parked`, but the migration plan says cards now tap through to `/race/ios/[stepId]` after the 2026-05-15 summary-surface cutover. Export debt is high because the detail surface is now paired with a shipped summary surface.

exports.source: Claude Design — Race Prep cards iOS / timeline-grid summary iOS handoff
exports.notes: Migration plan says this handoff landed and the cards-grid cutover shipped on 2026-05-15. The cutover checklist names the four-state card grammar explicitly. This is the clearest P0 documentation gap.

exports.source: Claude Design — On the Water iOS atmospheric capture surface
exports.notes: Fresh-build live-capture surface. Migration plan calls out atmospheric-ground treatment and hero-mic composer; compliance audit says canonical design remains in Claude Design.

exports.source: Claude Design — Debrief iOS chronological reflection surface
exports.notes: Migration plan distinguishes this student-facing reflection surface from the separate Competency Assessment artifact. Cross-cutting audit flags missing exported loading and error states for the AI clustering offer.

exports.source: Claude Design — Playbook home iOS library surface
exports.notes: This is the only surface marked `canonical_status=shipped` in the JSON inventory. Export debt is high because the canonical cutover already happened.

exports.source: Claude Design — Concept detail iOS surface (`Read / Work on Concept` segmented-control family)
exports.notes: Migration plan inventory explicitly names `Read mode`; the segmented-control rule references `Read / Work on Concept iOS`, and the compliance audit flags a resynthesize loading/error path. Variant naming should be normalized against the Claude artifact at export time.

exports.source: Claude Design — Reflect home iOS preview surface
exports.notes: Migration plan says Reflect cutover remains blocked on Race Log iOS and Profile iOS handoffs plus reflection-usage tracking. Export the parent surface and the two child handoffs as separate artifacts.

exports.source: Claude Design — Race Log iOS handoff for Reflect sub-tab
exports.notes: Missing from the JSON inventory but explicitly called out in the migration-plan handoff backlog as required for Reflect cutover.

exports.source: Claude Design — Profile iOS handoff for Reflect sub-tab
exports.notes: A repo brief exists, but the migration plan still counts the canonical handoff as outstanding for Reflect cutover. Cross-cutting audit marks loading not applicable and error states still undesigned.

exports.source: Claude Design — Discover Paths iOS top-level Discover surface
exports.notes: Inventory covers only the top-level Discover Paths surface, while the migration plan adds three deeper Discover handoffs still missing from inventory.
Explicitly listed in the migration-plan backlog as blocking Discover cutover. No dedicated route is documented in the JSON inventory yet.
Explicitly listed in the migration-plan backlog as blocking Discover cutover. No dedicated route is documented in the JSON inventory yet.
Explicitly listed in the migration-plan backlog as blocking Discover cutover. No dedicated route is documented in the JSON inventory yet.

exports.source: Claude Design — Get Inspired iOS modal sheet plus running-state handoff
exports.notes: The repo brief covers only the running state explicitly. The migration plan says empty and filled already exist, so export should capture all three states as one canonical surface bundle.

exports.source: Claude Design — Trophy of Becoming iOS synthesis artifact
exports.notes: Task context says this surface has four variants, but the cross-referenced repo docs do not name them. Preserve the four-export expectation and normalize variant labels when the Claude Design artifact is exported.

exports.source: Claude Design — Step transition hinge iOS surface
exports.notes: Placeholder tiles are wired in preview, but the compliance audit still treats canonical design as Claude-only because no export exists in the repo.

exports.source: Claude Design — Auth Welcome iOS pre-auth surface
exports.notes: Visual-only preview surface. Cross-cutting audit marks both loading narration and BetterAt-specific error treatment as not applicable, but the canonical design still lacks a repo export.

exports.source: Claude Design — Competency Assessment iOS faculty surface
exports.notes: Migration plan says the designed surface only specified the populated state; `0 captures` empty-state copy remains unresolved, and submit flow plus AI capture surfacing are still deferred. Export the faculty handoff now, then add empty/error variants later if Claude Design produces them.
